key_points: Stevenson elevators reportedly got stuck 27 times in one semester
Neptune rooms are notably small with no dressers included
False fire alarms at ungodly hours are a recurring dorm problem
key_quotes: Don't stay in the towers: Stevenson elevators got stuck 27 times last semester and grant ones get stuck at the same time
Neptune rooms are small, so maybe aim for Stevenson or Grant if you can
Patterson East has had 2 fire alarms go off at ungodly hours of the morning so far this semester

key_points: Campus is essentially dead on weekends — most students go home
Students actively creating clubs just to fill the social void
Making friends as a transfer or commuter is genuinely difficult
key_quotes: NIU is a commuter college now. No one stays for anything and even the professors are too lazy to do anything
It is so freaking boring on the weekends because no one does anything
Campus is Pretty much dead on the weekends. Everyone goes home

key_points: Gilbert Dining Hall closed and students are petitioning to reopen it
Gilbert was the only close-style dining hall — its loss is widely mourned
Staffing cuts and low enrollment cited as reasons dining was reduced
key_quotes: Oh man they still haven't reopened it? That's such a shame, it's such a unique dining hall. It legit felt like a hotel brunch room
I was SHOCKED to learn that Gilbert had their dining services cut
The only way that will happen is if enrollment spikes significantly

key_points: Students report bullying and name-calling incidents on campus buses
Social isolation is common, especially for transfers and commuters
Campus culture described as bland and emotionally hollow by long-term students
key_quotes: Twice now I've had groups of people either making fun of me or my friends, and actively calling people names...I was called names for almost 10 minutes
The culture in this campus feels bland...there's a hole within the heart of NIU that I feel is missing
I've been at this school for over a year, mostly just cramming...I don't have many friends

key_points: Financial aid office is infamous for ignoring calls, emails, and walk-ins
System errors have accidentally revoked student loans mid-semester
NIU offers very competitive aid packages — a major reason students choose it
key_quotes: I've left emails, ignored, tried to call, was on hold for 30 minutes...this guy did not make eye contact or say anything for like 10 minutes
I received an email this afternoon saying that my loans were accidentally revoked
NIU's cost WITH Federal Student Loans: $1.6k [vs UIC's $18k]

key_points: Psychology and law programs earn genuine praise from students and alumni
NIU's CS program described as 'mediocre' by students comparing options
Blackboard and tech infrastructure cause recurring academic disruptions
key_quotes: NIU alumni who majored in psych, nothing but great things to say about the program!!! The professors are very kind
Northern Illinois University (which has a mediocre CS Program)
I missed an entire lesson on zoom yesterday because the wifi dropped so bad

key_points: Financial aid office widely criticized for unresponsiveness and errors
Physical OneCards eliminated — mobile-only policy frustrates students with dying phones
Elevator safety inspection permits expired mid-semester with no urgency to fix
key_quotes: Is it just me or does financial aid go out of its way to ignore you?
They said they don't do physical cards anymore — just the mobile one
The safety inspection permits for the elevators expired at the end of August...elevators have yet to be reevaluated

key_points: DeKalb is a small, quiet town — chain restaurants, Walmart, not much else
Campus WiFi is unreliable with outages affecting entire academic days
Annie Glidden Road is dangerously dark and poorly maintained at night
key_quotes: The town is dead. To be exact honest with you!
Pages taking forever to load, random internet outages, buildings with low bars/no signal, and constantly seeing the dinosaur game
The entire road is a deathtrap. The speed limit is 30 but everyone speeds

key_points: Law school alumni report solid job placement and strong public interest reputation
NIU accounting and business programs have a decent regional employer base
Perception as a lower-tier school may hurt job searches outside Illinois
key_quotes: I've known a small handful of NIU law grads...all got hired on to decent jobs quickly. 5 of 7 passed the bar on the first time
Big alumni base in the government and collar counties
NIU isn't an amazing school so I assume it would be pretty hard [to transfer to Michigan]

key_points: NIU's aid packages are genuinely exceptional — often nearly free for in-state students
Low cost makes it a serious contender even against stronger schools
Full rides are common enough that students routinely compare NIU vs. UIUC on cost alone
key_quotes: NIU's cost WITH Federal Student Loans: $1.6k
Go NIU. Trust me, housing kills in costs so take the break when you can
I got a full ride at Northern Illinois University

key_points: Commuter school energy dominates — campus empties every weekend
Alumni nostalgia is strong, but current students feel the culture has eroded
Small pockets of genuine community exist in music, clubs, and the nature trail
key_quotes: NIU is a commuter college now. No one stays for anything
Everyday since graduating, I just dream of being back in Dekalb. I miss my undergrad years
I've been living on-campus all 4 years and there's one thing I've noticed: the culture in this campus feels bland

key_points: **Financial Aid**: office ignores students for weeks, has made errors revoking loans mid-semester
**Campus Safety**: Annie Glidden Road dangerously unlit; ICE activity confirmed near campus in unmarked vehicles
**Cult Recruitment**: Brightfield Church actively targets NIU students; university has formally warned about it
key_quotes: Stay away from Brightfield church. They are a high control cult that will suck you in with their 'love bombing'...they will encourage you to cut off your family
ICE agents kidnapped a person on their way to work [near campus]
I walked into the office, walked up to the front desk...this guy did not make eye contact or say anything for like 10 minutes

key_points: DeKalb has a thriving DIY punk/indie/emo music scene centered around Fargo venue
Nature trail along the Kishwaukee River is genuinely beautiful and underused
Law school is a standout — affordable, small classes, strong faculty access
key_quotes: NIU students get 50% off [Fargo shows] with OneCard!
Yesterday was a beautiful day for a walk, so i went on the nature trail and took a few photos
It's a great school. It's small, so you get to know faculty, staff, and students. Cheap, so you don't graduate debt
